description: Most of my work happens across both frontend and backend.
I enjoy building APIs almost as much as building interfaces.
Usually the person fixing bugs and deploying features on the same day.
Spent more time in terminals than I probably should admit.
I like keeping things simple, even when the system behind it is not.
Servers, databases, APIs, UI — I work across the whole stack.
A lot of my projects start with "this should be quick".
Sometimes I redesign a component five times before shipping it.
Currently obsessed with making websites feel faster.
I care about clean code, but I care even more about maintainable code.
Most production issues happen at the most interesting times.
Still excited every time a feature works on the first try.
100% Job Success Score. Still trying to keep it that way.
Earned Upwork Rising Talent early in my freelance journey.
Received 5-star feedback from every completed project so far.
I enjoy turning complex requirements into simple user experiences.
Usually thinking about performance before anyone asks.
Building things from scratch is still my favorite part of development.
The backend deserves just as much attention as the UI.
Always learning something new between deployments.
The best projects are the ones that keep helping clients long after launch.
I enjoy seeing a product grow from an idea into something people use every day.
A successful launch is nice. Sustainable growth is even better.
Most clients come with a problem. The code is usually the easy part.
I like building things that save time, reduce effort, or create new opportunities.
Good software should help a business move faster, not create more work.
The goal is rarely just a website. Usually it is growth, automation, or better experiences.
Some of my favorite projects started with a simple idea and evolved into something much bigger.
I enjoy helping founders focus on their business while the technology quietly does its job.
The most rewarding part is seeing a solution make a real difference for the people using it.
